title: Dryopteris khasiana
taxonomy: Dryopteris khasiana is classified within the family Dryopteridaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the wood ferns. This species is further categorized under the genus Dryopteris, which consists of various species of wood ferns found in different parts of the world. The genus Dryopteris is itself part of the larger family Dryopteridaceae, which is one of the several families within the order Polypodiales. intro: Dryopteris khasiana, also known as Khasian shield fern, is a species of fern native to the Himalayan region. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various diseases and promote overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris khasiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or tincture to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
